tool_args.label: Staff Summary - Amendment to Viaduct Agreement with JPMorgan Chase

payload.title: Staff Summary - Amendment to Viaduct Agreement with JPMorgan Chase related to Redevelopment of 270 Park Avenue
payload.description: Board staff summary seeking authorization to execute an Early Work Agreement and Sector 2 Amendment to the Viaduct Agreement with JPMorgan Chase for rehabilitation of the Grand Central Terminal Train Shed.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed Sector 2 Rehabilitation
tool_args.raw_text: JPMC has agreed to expand the scope of its ongoing rehabilitation work on the Train Shed on behalf of the MTA Parties, to now include 46th Street between Madison and Park Avenues, Vanderbilt Avenue between 46th and 47th Streets, Park Avenue between 46th and 50th Streets (excluding a portion that JPMC already completed), 49th Street between Park Avenue and the midway point between Park and Madison Avenues, and certain partial spurs off of Park Avenue on 46th, 47th, 48th, and 49th Streets East towards Lexington Avenue (collectively, the \"Expanded Scope\" or the \"Project\").

payload.project_name: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed Sector 2 Rehabilitation
payload.description: Amendment to Viaduct Agreement with JPMorgan Chase for rehabilitation of a portion of the Grand Central Terminal Train Shed (Sector 2 / Expanded Scope) including streets between 46th and 50th Streets around Park Avenue

tool_args.label: Board Approved JPMC Sector 1 Agreement
tool_args.raw_text: On March 25, 2019, the Board approved an agreement with JPMC, which was developing its new headquarters building at 270 Park Avenue, New York, NY, located over a portion of the Train Shed

payload.description: Board approved an agreement with JPMC for development at 270 Park Avenue over a portion of the Train Shed

tool_args.label: Viaduct Construction Agreement Executed
tool_args.raw_text: Viaduct Construction Agreement, executed on October 20, 2021 (the 'Viaduct Agreement')

payload.description: Viaduct Construction Agreement executed between JPMC and MTA Parties for Sector 1 Train Shed repair

tool_args.label: Expansion LOI Signed
tool_args.raw_text: JPMC and the MTA Parties entered into a Letter of Intent ('Expansion LOI') dated September 20, 2024

payload.description: JPMC and MTA Parties entered into a Letter of Intent (Expansion LOI) for JPMC to manage additional portion of the Project (Expanded Scope) and contribute $50M

tool_args.label: Sector 2 Anticipated Completion
tool_args.raw_text: The Sector 2 work has an anticipated duration of 36 months, with an anticipated completion date of May 30, 2029.

payload.description: Anticipated completion date for Sector 2 work of the Grand Central Terminal Train Shed rehabilitation

tool_args.label: MTA Phase 2 Election Deadline
tool_args.raw_text: MTA must elect to do by June 15, 2026

payload.description: Deadline for MTA to elect to direct JPMC to perform Phase 2 (north of 48th Street) of the Expanded Scope

tool_args.label: NYC DOT Park Avenue Malls Plans Deadline
tool_args.raw_text: If the New York City Department of Transportation does not provide final plans for its Park Avenue Malls project by December 31, 2026

payload.description: If NYC DOT does not provide final plans for its Park Avenue Malls project by December 31, 2026, the Expanded Work will proceed in a manner enabling NYC DOT to rebuild the affected portion of the Park Avenue Malls once plans are complete

tool_args.label: Total budget approximately $900M
tool_args.raw_text: The budget for this Work is estimated to be approximately $900M

payload.context: MTA reasonably expects that the total cost for Phase 1 and Phase 2 will be approximately $900M

tool_args.label: JPMC will contribute $50M
tool_args.raw_text: JPMC will contribute $50 million towards the cost of the Project

payload.scope: JPMC contribution to Expanded Scope costs

tool_args.raw_text: JPMC's Sector 1 work is 95% complete

tool_args.raw_text: with a completion date scheduled for December 2026

tool_args.raw_text: JPMC will pay for 100% of the cost of such work capped at $50M

payload.scope: Phase 1 work under Early Work Agreement (below 48th Street)

tool_args.label: Sector 2 work duration 36 months
tool_args.raw_text: The Sector 2 work has an anticipated duration of 36 months

payload.scope: Sector 2 work (Expanded Scope north of 48th Street)

tool_args.label: JPMC contributed $25M for Sector 1
tool_args.raw_text: JPMC agreed to repair the portion of the Train Shed surrounding 270 Park Avenue... and contribute $25 Million toward the performance of such work.

tool_args.raw_text: with a completion date scheduled for December 2026

issues: metric_claim payload with raw_value_text should include normalized numeric value or value_min/value_max

tool_args.label: Special provisions: MTA/C&D/MNR review authority for Sector 2
tool_args.raw_text: all JPMC designs, contractor submittals, means and methods, protection plans, security and safety plans, project schedules, and insurance for Sector 2 work are subject to MTA, C&D and/or MNR review and approval prior to the start of construction. Additionally, JPMC is required to protect MNR facilities in order to maintain and prevent or mitigate interruption of railroad operations.

payload.claim_text: All JPMC designs, contractor submittals, means and methods, protection plans, security and safety plans, project schedules, and insurance for Sector 2 work are subject to MTA, C&D and/or MNR review and approval prior to the start of construction. JPMC is required to protect MNR facilities in order to maintain and prevent or mitigate interruption of railroad operations.
payload.subject: Sector 2 work

tool_args.label: Remainder funded by 2025-2029 Capital Program
tool_args.raw_text: JPMC will fund $50 million towards the cost of the Project and the remainder will be funded by the 2025-2029 Capital Program.

payload.claim_text: JPMC will fund $50 million towards the cost of the Project and the remainder will be funded by the 2025-2029 Capital Program.
payload.subject: Project funding

tool_args.label: Train Shed described as bridge supporting at-grade streets
tool_args.raw_text: The Train Shed constitutes the roof of Grand Central Terminal train platforms and supports Park Avenue between 46th and 57th Streets and certain side streets radiating therefrom. The Train Shed, in effect, constitutes a bridge supporting the at-grade streets and avenues and is, in turn, supported by steel columns and other structures that were originally constructed in the early 20th Century.

payload.claim_text: The Train Shed constitutes the roof of Grand Central Terminal train platforms and supports Park Avenue between 46th and 57th Streets and certain side streets radiating therefrom. The Train Shed, in effect, constitutes a bridge supporting the at-grade streets and avenues and is, in turn, supported by steel columns and other structures that were originally constructed in the early 20th Century. The structural steel that supports the Train Shed has significantly deteriorated over time.
payload.subject: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed

tool_args.label: Public-private partnerships with adjacent building owners
tool_args.raw_text: Because the private building owners along the streets and avenues supported by the Train Shed will benefit from the rehabilitation project, the MTA Parties have engaged in a series of public-private partnerships with adjacent building owners to jointly manage the Train Shed rehabilitation project, including JPMC.

payload.claim_text: Because the private building owners along the streets and avenues supported by the Train Shed will benefit from the rehabilitation project, the MTA Parties have engaged in a series of public-private partnerships with adjacent building owners to jointly manage the Train Shed rehabilitation project, including JPMC.
payload.subject: Train Shed rehabilitation

tool_args.label: Project implemented by MTA Parties
tool_args.raw_text: AGENCIES: MTA Construction and Development Company ('C&D'), Metropolitan Transportation Authority ('MTA'), and Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company ('MNR'; and together with C&D and MTA, collectively, the 'MTA Parties')

payload.description: MTA Parties lead the Train Shed rehabilitation project

tool_args.label: Project has partner JPMC
tool_args.raw_text: the MTA Parties have engaged in a series of public-private partnerships with adjacent building owners to jointly manage the Train Shed rehabilitation project, including JPMC.

payload.description: JPMC as public-private partner for Train Shed rehabilitation

tool_args.label: Project coordinates with NYC DOT on Park Avenue Malls
tool_args.raw_text: The Expansion LOI further provides for coordination with the New York City Department of Transportation (the 'DOT') in carrying out a project to rehabilitate and reconfigure the Park Avenue Malls, as part of the Project.

payload.description: Coordination with NYC DOT for Park Avenue Malls rehabilitation and reconfiguration project

payload.description: MTA must elect by June 15, 2026 to direct JPMC to perform Phase 2 work

tool_args.label: Park Avenue between 46th and 50th Streets (Sector 2 area)
tool_args.raw_text: An approximate 183,500 square foot portion of the Grand Central Terminal Train Shed (the 'Train Shed') underneath Park Avenue and on certain side streets extending easterly and westerly on Park Avenue from East 46th Street to East 50th Street, New York, NY, further referenced as 'Sector 2'.

payload.description: Approximate 183,500 square foot portion of the Grand Central Terminal Train Shed underneath Park Avenue and on certain side streets extending easterly and westerly on Park Avenue from East 46th Street to East 50th Street

tool_args.label: Project includes Sector 2 corridor
tool_args.raw_text: JPMC has agreed to expand the scope of its ongoing rehabilitation work on the Train Shed on behalf of the MTA Parties, to now include 46th Street between Madison and Park Avenues, Vanderbilt Avenue between 46th and 47th Streets, Park Avenue between 46th and 50th Streets

payload.description: The expanded scope for Train Shed rehabilitation includes Park Avenue between 46th and 50th Streets and adjacent side streets

tool_args.label: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed steel rehabilitation
tool_args.raw_text: The structural steel that supports the Train Shed has significantly deteriorated over time and the MTA Parties have embarked on a long-term project to rehabilitate these steel structures to ensure the viability of both the Train Shed and the streets and sidewalks that it supports.

payload.description: Rehabilitation of deteriorated structural steel supporting the Grand Central Terminal Train Shed, which constitutes a bridge supporting at-grade streets and avenues
